name: St. Claude Bridge — Piling Access and Skiff
description: The maintenance ladder on the bridge's eastern face descends to a concrete ledge at water level where the skiff is moored; the crate stenciled with a red cross and a pharmaceutical lot number sits on the bow, and Devereux Molina watches the bridge deck from below.
atmosphericDetails: The water at piling level is the same grey-brown as the water everywhere — fuel iridescence visible in a thin film on the surface near the motor
The crate is stenciled with lot number consistent with a saline and electrolyte package; the sealed secondary package inside is not visible from the bridge deck
The canal surface reflects the flat white-yellow sky without distortion; there are no ripples except from the idling motor
The smell at water level is more chemical than at deck level — the petroleum hydrocarbon component is stronger here
